strengths: Direct recent experience contributing to AI-agent infrastructure and developer tooling (MCP code generators, agentic workflows) at re:cinq, aligning closely with the job’s focus on multi-agent AI systems

Deep backend and distributed-systems expertise demonstrated across high-scale environments (e.g., Dunnhumby media handling millions of HTTP requests/sec, microservices, event-driven architectures, and real-time systems)

Strong track record of scaling engineering practices and tooling (e.g., setting up CI/CD, GitLab instances, and dev-ops infrastructure at Altom Consulting), with a focus on disciplined, testable, and scalable systems
concerns: Lacks explicit fintech or financial-domain experience (e.g., credit risk, trading systems, or embedded finance), which is a hard requirement for this role
Limited evidence of hands-on architectural leadership in regulated or high-stakes commercial environments (e.g., no explicit mention of compliance, auditability, or multi-jurisdictional systems)
Resume does not highlight experience with real-time inference systems, streaming data, or production ML feature engineering, which are core technical expectations
response.justification: Bogdan demonstrates exceptional alignment with the core technical architecture and engineering leadership requirements for this role, particularly in AI-agent systems and distributed infrastructure. His recent work on AI-agent tooling and frameworks at re:cinq directly matches the job’s emphasis on modular, event-driven multi-agent architectures and observability. Additionally, his extensive experience scaling backend systems in high-throughput environments (e.g., Dunnhumby) and establishing disciplined engineering practices (e.g., CI/CD, testing) underscores his readiness to lead a technical organization. While his fintech-specific background is not explicitly documented, his adaptability and depth in adjacent domains (e.g., real-time systems, microservices) suggest strong upside for rapid upskilling in financial infrastructure.
